title: carex continua var. plebeia
intro: Carex continua var. plebeia, also known as the blue-green sedge, is a type of perennial grass that belongs to the sedge family and is native to eastern North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe and heal wounds. The health benefits of Carex continua var. plebeia include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory health, and aid in digestion. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, which can be used to treat a variety of health issues, including colds, fever, and digestive problems.
